posts.name: Philip Laub
posts.cooked: I recently acquired a few bottles each of the 2012 and 2104 Roulot Boucheres. I have zero experience with Roulot’s wines, and based the purchases entirely upon his reputation. Any feedback/thoughts about these two wines would be greatly appreciated.

I sell these wines so take this with a grain of salt…

They are delicious, great acid and cut with really ethereal weight. There is a post in the past day where someone had the 14’, you might want to look it up.

The wines can age as well…

posts.name: Mike Grammer
posts.cooked:
If you end up disappointed, Philip, that will be disappointing to us all. Roulot’s wines are fantastic. I have never had the chance to try a Boucheres but can only imagine. The vintages you have, if you open them anytime soon, give them lots and lots of air first, would be my recommendation.

Belated welcome to the board!

Mike

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: The Clos des Bouchères is a lot better than the Bouchères that preceded it. I think it’s probably the second-best wine in the cellar after the Perrières, better than his Charmes.

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked: I have had a number of premoxed Roulot. I would drink them young.

posts.name: Philip Laub
posts.cooked:
“I have had a number of premoxed Roulot. I would drink them young.”

Wondering which vintages or at what age these premox’ed bottles are?

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked: They should be spectacular. In my (admittedly limited) experience with Roulot, the Boucheres is the sweet spot of the entire range.

posts.name: Robert Pavlovich
posts.cooked:
Philip Laub:

“I have had a number of premoxed Roulot. I would drink them young.”

Wondering which vintages or at what age these premox’ed bottles are?

Based on a recent tasting of the 14’ CdBoucheres, I’d recommend holding 3-4 years.

posts.name: Mike Grammer
posts.cooked:
I’m curious too, Kevin. I’ve had great luck with Roulots, no premoxed bottles at all

Mike

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: Personally I like the clos boucheres more than the charmes. Great cut and acid. The 14 is an absolutely ethereal wine.

posts.name: Nate DiMaggio
posts.cooked:
Philip Laub:

“I have had a number of premoxed Roulot. I would drink them young.”

Wondering which vintages or at what age these premox’ed bottles are?

I have had a scary amount of pre moxed wines from Roulot…he stil hasn’t fixed the problem either. I have drank quite a few bottles thry 14 & 15 (from Perrieres down) and most newer vintages have been amazing but even some 14’s and 15’s have showed some of that bruised yellow apple on the palate. Bottle variation as with anything…

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked:
The only vintage I can think of with roulot with severe oxidation problems is 02 perrieres. Everyone’s mileage may vary of course. I’ve had a retailer earn me off buying 10 roulot perrieres from him due to some bruised apple but I’ve drank at least 6 btls of 10 roulot mp and they’ve all been great.

Shrug. I trust Kevin and Nate’s palates, I just fortunately haven’t had any issues.

posts.name: Nick Gangas
posts.cooked: I’m convinced some of the premix problem derives from storage and transport.

posts.name: Larry Link
posts.cooked: I’ve consumed 76 bottles of Roulot from the 2005 vintage to the 2015 vintage inclusive. Of the 76 only 2 were advanced, one from 05 and one from 07. Based on my personal experience, I’m not convinced that Roulot has a Pre-mox problem; unlike Leflaive, Jadot or Lafon who have major issues.

posts.name: Laurent Gibet
posts.cooked:
I tasted many wines at the domain last november …
No premox

Best wines tasted :
Roulot Meursault Perrières 2016 : 18/20
Roulot Meursault Tillets 2011 : 17,5/20
Roulot Meursault Luchets 2007 : 17/20

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
Nick Gangas:

I’m convinced some of the premix problem derives from storage and transport.

That is possible.

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
Larry Link:

I’ve consumed 76 bottles of Roulot from the 2005 vintage to the 2015 vintage inclusive. Of the 76 only 2 were advanced, one from 05 and one from 07. Based on my personal experience, I’m not convinced that Roulot has a Pre-mox problem; unlike Leflaive, Jadot or Lafon who have major issues.

Lafon since 2009 is making great wines. I have not had any premox issue since 2009.

posts.name: Nick Gangas
posts.cooked:
I love Lafon wines but will admit I’m scared.

Anecdotally it seems that premix sometimes seems to be clustered. I’ve had very little issue with Roulot myself.

suggested_topics.title: TN: 1995 E. Guigal Condrieu (France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Condrieu)
suggested_topics.excerpt:
1995 E. Guigal Condrieu - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Condrieu (7/6/2025) 
Not dead! Although more intriguing than tasty, it did show some nice aged character...stewed apricot, sour grapefruit, peach pie...antique wax…
